title: Greatest Common Divisor
description: Given two integers a and b, return their greatest common divisor (GCD). The GCD of two numbers is the largest positive integer that divides both numbers without leaving a remainder.

examples.explanation: The divisors of 48 are {1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 12, 16, 24, 48} and the divisors of 18 are {1, 2, 3, 6, 9, 18}. The largest common divisor is 6.
The divisors of 56 are {1, 2, 4, 7, 8, 14, 28, 56} and the divisors of 98 are {1, 2, 7, 14, 49, 98}. The largest common divisor is 14.
The only positive integer that divides both 101 and 10 is 1, thus the GCD is 1.

hints: Consider using the Euclidean algorithm for an efficient solution.
You can reduce the problem by replacing the larger number with its remainder when divided by the smaller number.
Think about how to handle very large numbers within the constraints.
Explore built-in functions in your programming language that might simplify this task.
